AccessDeniedPath example — split into LoginPath + AccessDeniedPath with separate comments

docFactsUsed: AREA-009: ContentItemFieldsSourceExtensions.HasAccess(this IContentItemFieldsSource, ClaimsPrincipal) — public extension method returning bool
AREA-009: Three-tier access check: Allowed (200), NotAuthenticated (401), Forbidden (403) via internal ContentAccessCheckResult enum
AREA-009: Content access cookie auth configuration separates login path from access denied path; auto-fallback when LoginPath not set
AREA-009: MemberAccessHelper.CheckAccess logic: !isSecured→Allowed, !authenticated→NotAuthenticated, no required roles→Allowed, user in any role→Allowed, else→Forbidden
AREA-006: ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames: IReadOnlyCollection<string> available on query result containers
AREA-006: For unsecured items, ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames returns empty collection
notes: Consolidated IMP-006 and IMP-016 into a single update. The three-tier model (public/secured/secured-with-roles) replaces the previous binary secured/unsecured description. HasAccess() extension documented as the recommended programmatic check per REC-014 progression (simple→complex). The backward-compatible path handling callout reflects the actual ContentAccessCookieAuthenticationConfiguration behavior from source code: when LoginPath is not set but AccessDeniedPath is, LoginPath falls back to AccessDeniedPath. Cross-references added to member_roles_xp (TASK-001) and reference_content_access_settings_xp (TASK-002).
